title: There are no lossless transformations of natural-language text
excerpt: In my work at Clay I recently wrote an internal policy on acceptable use of AI writing by engineers, and I’m sharing it here. It’s my hope that one day better AI tools might be able to help us think, but until then I fear that using AI to write does the exact opposite.
content_html:
In my work at Clay I recently wrote an internal policy on acceptable use of AI writing by engineers, and I’m sharing it here. It’s my hope that one day better AI tools might be able to help us think, but until then I fear that using AI to write does the exact opposite.

Good writing is a tool to clearly communicate ideas from your brain into someone else’s.

As of 2026, AI models — despite their coding ability — are not yet at a point where their unedited output will achieve this goal; you as an author need to take the time to make sure that all of the ideas in the writing are the ideas that you personally intend to convey (including the structure and wording that determines which ideas are emphasized) and that the documents are a good use of your readers’ time.

It’s allowed to use AI tools while brainstorming or drafting your writing and certainly while proofreading, but make sure to consider the following principles while doing so:

  • You must stand behind every idea and every sentence in your docs. It is your responsibility to make sure that the entire document is representative of your own thoughts before you share it. If a reviewer asks, “What did you mean by this line?”, it’s not acceptable to reply with “Oh sorry, AI wrote that, just ignore it.” You will confuse your readers (and waste their time) if you present them things that are not genuinely representative of your thoughts. In some cases, readers will recognize and call out the incongruous parts; in others, they will be misled as to what your actual thoughts are.

  • Writing is thinking. Spending time on the writing process — on deciding what to emphasize and how to structure your ideas clearly — teaches you more about your topic. If you circumvent this process, you will probably walk away with a poorer understanding of the subject matter. In many cases, written artifacts like tech specs, project status updates, and incident retrospectives serve as a “proof of thought”. The artifact itself is not the only goal; instead, detailed thinking about the problem is the goal. Outsourcing the creation of these documents to AI (so that you can skip the thinking) risks circumventing this very purpose. Even if working with AI helps you think through a problem, you’ll understand it better if you thoroughly review the result yourself.

  • More time should be spent authoring a document than consuming it. If you generate a document from a short prompt then ask your readers to go through the longer output, you are disrespecting their time. They can always talk to ChatGPT themselves if they want to. Most docs are written by one person but are read by many people, so any extra time that readers need to spend to understand what you meant incurs a multiplicative cost on the team’s time. Conversely, if you spend extra time to make your document clear and concise before sending it, you are paying a one-time cost that every reader will benefit from.

  • Longer is not better. Pascal once wrote, “I have made this [letter] longer than usual because I have not had time to make it shorter.” AI makes it much easier to generate a long doc, and one of its strategies is to include many sentences that don’t say much at all and detract from the actual content. If you are producing a longer piece of writing from a shorter prompt, consider instead just sharing the prompt itself. If you use AI to edit without lengthening, vacuous sentences are less of a risk but your meaning may still get obscured.

    There are no lossless transformations of natural-language text — every rewrite and rephrase changes the meaning of your writing, and if this is done by an entity that doesn’t have the most detailed mental representation of what you personally were trying to communicate, information will be lost. Readers will appreciate hearing your thoughts, even at the expense of supposed “polish”.

It’s also OK to quote AI generations verbatim that don’t meet the above standards if you mark them clearly as such. Sometimes it’s useful to say like “Claude offered this idea, do you think it’s worth looking more into?”, and this is allowed.

As AI tools improve over time at theory of mind and get better at writing, it may make sense to lean more heavily on them, but the principles above will remain important.

blocks.html_en: In my work at Clay I recently wrote an internal policy on acceptable use of AI writing by engineers, and I’m sharing it here. It’s my hope that one day better AI tools might be able to help us think, but until then I fear that using AI to write does the exact opposite.

sentences.en: In my work at Clay I recently wrote an internal policy on acceptable use of AI writing by engineers, and I’m sharing it here.
sentences.zh: 在 Clay 的工作中，我最近编写了一项关于工程师可接受的人工智能写作使用的内部政策，我在这里分享它。

sentences.en: It’s my hope that one day better AI tools might be able to help us think, but until then I fear that using AI to write does the exact opposite.
sentences.zh: 我希望有一天更好的人工智能工具能够帮助我们思考，但在那之前我担心使用人工智能写作会适得其反。

blocks.html_en: Good writing is a tool to clearly communicate ideas from your brain into someone else’s.

sentences.en: Good writing is a tool to clearly communicate ideas from your brain into someone else’s.
sentences.zh: 好的写作是一种将你的想法清晰地传达给别人的工具。

blocks.html_en: As of 2026, AI models — despite their coding ability — are not yet at a point where their unedited output will achieve this goal; you as an author need to take the time to make sure that all of the ideas in the writing are the ideas that you personally intend to convey (including the structure and wording that determines which ideas are emphasized) and that the documents are a good use of your readers’ time.

sentences.en: As of 2026, AI models — despite their coding ability — are not yet at a point where their unedited output will achieve this goal; you as an author need to take the time to make sure that all of the ideas in the writing are the ideas that you personally intend to convey (including the structure and wording that determines which ideas are emphasized) and that the documents are a good use of your readers’ time.
sentences.zh: 截至 2026 年，人工智能模型尽管具有编码能力，但其未经编辑的输出尚未达到这一目标；作为作者，您需要花时间确保写作中的所有想法都是您个人想要传达的想法（包括决定强调哪些想法的结构和措辞），并且这些文档充分利用了读者的时间。

blocks.html_en: It’s allowed to use AI tools while brainstorming or drafting your writing and certainly while proofreading, but make sure to consider the following principles while doing so:

sentences.en: It’s allowed to use AI tools while brainstorming or drafting your writing and certainly while proofreading, but make sure to consider the following principles while doing so:
sentences.zh: 在集思广益或起草写作时以及校对时，可以使用人工智能工具，但请务必在这样做时考虑以下原则：

blocks.html_en: • You must stand behind every idea and every sentence in your docs. It is your responsibility to make sure that the entire document is representative of your own thoughts before you share it. If a reviewer asks, “What did you mean by this line?”, it’s not acceptable to reply with “Oh sorry, AI wrote that, just ignore it.” You will confuse your readers (and waste their time) if you present them things that are not genuinely representative of your thoughts. In some cases, readers will recognize and call out the incongruous parts; in others, they will be misled as to what your actual thoughts are.

sentences.en: You must stand behind every idea and every sentence in your docs.
sentences.zh: 您必须支持文档中的每个想法和每个句子。

sentences.en: It is your responsibility to make sure that the entire document is representative of your own thoughts before you share it.
sentences.zh: 在分享之前，您有责任确保整个文档代表您自己的想法。

sentences.en: If a reviewer asks, “What did you mean by this line?”, it’s not acceptable to reply with “Oh sorry, AI wrote that, just ignore it.”
sentences.zh: 如果审稿人问“你这句话是什么意思？”，则不能回答“哦，抱歉，这是人工智能写的，请忽略它。”

sentences.en: You will confuse your readers (and waste their time) if you present them things that are not genuinely representative of your thoughts.
sentences.zh: 如果你向读者展示的东西不能真正代表你的想法，你会让他们感到困惑（并浪费他们的时间）。

sentences.en: In some cases, readers will recognize and call out the incongruous parts; in others, they will be misled as to what your actual thoughts are.
sentences.zh: 在某些情况下，读者会认出并指出不协调的部分；在其他人中，他们会被误导，不知道你的真实想法是什么。

sentences.en: Outsourcing the creation of these documents to AI (so that you can skip the thinking) risks circumventing this very purpose.

sentences.zh: 如果您通过简短的提示生成文档，然后要求读者浏览较长的输出，那么您就是不尊重他们的时间。

sentences.en: They can always talk to ChatGPT themselves if they want to.
sentences.zh: 如果他们愿意，他们随时可以自己与 ChatGPT 交谈。

sentences.en: Most docs are written by one person but are read by many people, so any extra time that readers need to spend to understand what you meant incurs a multiplicative cost on the team’s time.

sentences.en: Conversely, if you spend extra time to make your document clear and concise before sending it, you are paying a one-time cost that every reader will benefit from.
sentences.zh: 相反，如果您在发送文档之前花费额外的时间使文档变得清晰简洁，那么您将付出一次性成本，而每个读者都会从中受益。

sentences.zh: 自然语言文本不存在无损转换——每次重写和改写都会改变你写作的含义，如果这是由一个对你个人试图传达的内容没有最详细心理表征的实体完成的，那么信息就会丢失。

sentences.en: Readers will appreciate hearing your thoughts, even at the expense of supposed “polish”.
sentences.zh: 读者会很高兴听到你的想法，即使是以牺牲所谓的“润色”为代价。

blocks.html_en: It’s also OK to quote AI generations verbatim that don’t meet the above standards if you mark them clearly as such. Sometimes it’s useful to say like “Claude offered this idea, do you think it’s worth looking more into?”, and this is allowed.

sentences.en: It’s also OK to quote AI generations verbatim that don’t meet the above standards if you mark them clearly as such.
sentences.zh: 如果您明确标记不符合上述标准的人工智能世代，也可以逐字引用。

sentences.en: Sometimes it’s useful to say like “Claude offered this idea, do you think it’s worth looking more into?”, and this is allowed.
sentences.zh: 有时可以这样说：“克劳德提出了这个想法，你认为它值得更多研究吗？”，这是允许的。

blocks.html_en: As AI tools improve over time at theory of mind and get better at writing, it may make sense to lean more heavily on them, but the principles above will remain important.

sentences.en: As AI tools improve over time at theory of mind and get better at writing, it may make sense to lean more heavily on them, but the principles above will remain important.
sentences.zh: 随着人工智能工具在心智理论方面的不断改进，以及在写作方面的进步，更多地依赖它们可能是有意义的，但上述原则仍然很重要。

content_html_zh:
在 Clay 的工作中，我最近编写了一项关于工程师可接受的人工智能写作使用的内部政策，我在这里分享它。 我希望有一天更好的人工智能工具能够帮助我们思考，但在那之前我担心使用人工智能写作会适得其反。

好的写作是一种将你的想法清晰地传达给别人的工具。

截至 2026 年，人工智能模型尽管具有编码能力，但其未经编辑的输出尚未达到这一目标；作为作者，您需要花时间确保写作中的所有想法都是您个人想要传达的想法（包括决定强调哪些想法的结构和措辞），并且这些文档充分利用了读者的时间。

在集思广益或起草写作时以及校对时，可以使用人工智能工具，但请务必在这样做时考虑以下原则：

  • 您必须支持文档中的每个想法和每个句子。 在分享之前，您有责任确保整个文档代表您自己的想法。 如果审稿人问“你这句话是什么意思？”，则不能回答“哦，抱歉，这是人工智能写的，请忽略它。” 如果你向读者展示的东西不能真正代表你的想法，你会让他们感到困惑（并浪费他们的时间）。 在某些情况下，读者会认出并指出不协调的部分；在其他人中，他们会被误导，不知道你的真实想法是什么。
  • 写作就是思考。 花时间在写作过程上——决定要强调什么以及如何清晰地构建你的想法——可以让你更多地了解你的主题。 如果你绕过这个过程，你可能会对主题的理解较差。 在许多情况下，技术规范、项目状态更新和事件回顾等书面工件可以作为“思想证明”。 神器本身并不是唯一的目标；相反，对问题的详细思考才是目标。 将这些文档的创建工作外包给人工智能（这样你就可以跳过思考）可能会规避这一目的。 即使使用人工智能可以帮助你思考问题，但如果你自己彻底检查结果，你会更好地理解它。
  • 应该花更多的时间来编写文档而不是使用它。 如果您通过简短的提示生成文档，然后要求读者浏览较长的输出，那么您就是不尊重他们的时间。 如果他们愿意，他们随时可以自己与 ChatGPT 交谈。 大多数文档都是由一个人编写，但有很多人阅读，因此读者需要花费额外的时间来理解您的意思，这会给团队的时间带来成倍的成本。 相反，如果您在发送文档之前花费额外的时间使文档变得清晰简洁，那么您将付出一次性成本，而每个读者都会从中受益。
  • 并不是越长越好。 帕斯卡曾经写道：“我把这封信写得比平时更长，因为我没有时间把它写短。” 人工智能使生成长文档变得更加容易，其策略之一是包含许多根本没有说太多内容并偏离实际内容的句子。 如果您要根据较短的提示写出较长的文章，请考虑仅共享提示本身。 如果你使用人工智能进行编辑而不加长，空洞的句子风险较小，但你的意思仍然可能会被模糊。 自然语言文本不存在无损转换——每次重写和改写都会改变你写作的含义，如果这是由一个对你个人试图传达的内容没有最详细心理表征的实体完成的，那么信息就会丢失。 读者会很高兴听到你的想法，即使是以牺牲所谓的“润色”为代价。

如果您明确标记不符合上述标准的人工智能世代，也可以逐字引用。 有时可以这样说：“克劳德提出了这个想法，你认为它值得更多研究吗？”，这是允许的。

随着人工智能工具在心智理论方面的不断改进，以及在写作方面的进步，更多地依赖它们可能是有意义的，但上述原则仍然很重要。
